title: History, Objects, References, and Forensic Inspection · Guided Repository Studio
summary: Explain when and why a behavior changed using graph, path, object, author, and content evidence. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of a history-forensics evidence packet while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Query commit graph topology, decorations, authorship, dates, messages, and changed paths with an intentional log format.
Relate blobs, trees, commits, tags, object IDs, content identity, and repository reachability.
Inspect commits, trees, blobs, tags, and object types without checking out or executing repository content.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-basics cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model
steps.instruction:
workshop case — History has commits a11ce00 → b00b1e5 → c0ffee2; a file moved from notes.txt to docs/notes.txt with unchanged bytes. Explain graph, object identity, path evidence, and blame limits.

What must be established before implementation?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for query commit graph topology, decorations, authorship, dates, messages, and changed paths with an intentional log format.

stimulus.title: Blame Context failure evidence

options.text: Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Blame Context condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.

steps.title: Defend the transferred model
steps.instruction: Write the workshop evidence note for Blame Context: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — History has commits a11ce00 → b00b1e5 → c0ffee6; a file moved from notes.txt to docs/notes.txt with unchanged bytes. Explain graph, object identity, path evidence, and blame limits.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Use blame as a line-origin clue and connect it to surrounding commits, review context, and later movement without assigning fault.
